question: Will the price of Ethereum be above $3,400 on January 4?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for XRP/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the XRP/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/XRP_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance XRP/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

question: Big Bash League: Perth Scorchers vs Adelaide Strikers (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Perth Scorchers and Adelaide Strikers scheduled for January 4 2026 in Big Bash League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

description:

description:

question: Big Bash League: Perth Scorchers vs Adelaide Strikers (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Perth Scorchers Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Perth Scorchers and Adelaide Strikers scheduled for 2026-01-04 in Big Bash League.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Perth Scorchers will be considered correct if  Perth Scorchers is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Adelaide Strikers will be considered correct if Adelaide Strikers is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: Big Bash League: Perth Scorchers vs Adelaide Strikers (Game 1) - Most Sixes Perth Scorchers Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Perth Scorchers and Adelaide Strikers scheduled for 2026-01-04 in Big Bash League.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Perth Scorchers will be considered correct if Perth Scorchers is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Adelaide Strikers.The outcome corresponding to Adelaide Strikers will be considered correct if Adelaide Strikers is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Perth Scorchers.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

description:

question: Big Bash League: Melbourne Stars vs Melbourne Renegades (Game 1) - Most Sixes Melbourne Stars Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Melbourne Stars and Melbourne Renegades scheduled for 2026-01-04 in Big Bash League.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Melbourne Stars will be considered correct if Melbourne Stars is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Melbourne Renegades.The outcome corresponding to Melbourne Renegades will be considered correct if Melbourne Renegades is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Melbourne Stars.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: SA20: MI Cape Town vs Paarl Royals (Game 1) - Most Sixes MI Cape Town Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between MI Cape Town and Paarl Royals scheduled for 2026-01-04 in SA20.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to MI Cape Town will be considered correct if MI Cape Town is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Paarl Royals.The outcome corresponding to Paarl Royals will be considered correct if Paarl Royals is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than MI Cape Town.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: Will Microstrategy announce a Bitcoin purchase December 30-January 5?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MicroStrategy Incorporated announces that they have acquired additional Bitcoin between the dates in the title (from 12:00 AM ET on the first date to 11:59 PM ET on the last date). Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve based on announcements made within the market's designated time frame regardless of when the actual purchases were made.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MicroStrategy or Michael Saylor.

For reference, MicroStrategy's reported BTC holdings can be tracked at: https://www.strategy.com/purchases

This market will resolve to "Yes" if MicroStrategy announces a purchase of more Bitcoin than the number specified in the title between the dates in the title (from 12:00 AM ET on the first date to 11:59 PM ET on the last date). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve based on announcements made within the market's designated time frame regardless of when the actual purchases were made.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MicroStrategy or Michael Saylor.

For reference, MicroStrategy's reported BTC holdings can be tracked at: https://www.strategy.com/purchases

question: Spread: FC Bayern München (-2.5)
description:
In the upcoming Bundesliga game, scheduled for January 11 at 11:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Bayern München" if FC Bayern München win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "VfL Wolfsburg".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on bundesliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.
